behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: taxonomy config + request builder + API client payload assembly. MOCK-MISS: this is not only one pure function if the behavior is about what is actually sent. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS needed; capture outbound payload with in-process stub client. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model decision quality. Rejected higher level System because behavior text is about request contents, not real HTTP conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a fake API client that records outbound request body; invoke classify with a known taxonomy and assert every valid label appears in the sent payload.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure is local normalization/validation logic on returned label. MOCK-MISS: no cross-module interaction is required to reproduce an out-of-taxonomy value. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none; use fixed input string. Rejected Integration because behavior text is explicitly the mapping rule from invalid label to "unknown", which is local logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call the label-normalization/response-mapping function with an invalid provider label and a taxonomy set; assert output is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is determinism of one normalization step. MOCK-MISS: no module boundary is needed if raw response is identical by construction. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none because response is fixed fixture input. Rejected Integration because behavior text scopes to "normalization step" itself.
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization repeatedly (or property-style) with the same raw response fixture and assert the final label is identical across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "repeated live API calls" and consistency conditioned on provider response changes, so correctness depends on real AI API outputs across runs. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing would force constant responses and hide provider-variance-related failures. REAL-INFRA is present, but the core risk is model output behavior, so Agentic is the tighter floor than System. Rejected Workflow because calls are repeated checks, not a multi-step agent pipeline with sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls for the same input; log raw provider responses and final labels; assert label changes occur only when raw provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration between config, tokenizer/counting utility, and request-dispatch gate. MOCK-MISS: a single isolated function test may miss wiring errors where budget check is skipped before dispatch. REAL-INFRA: real network/OS not required; fake dispatch is sufficient. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output. Rejected System because behavior text says enforcement happens "before dispatch."
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget, run request construction through the real module wiring with fake dispatcher, and assert policy is applied (truncate or reject) and outbound payload complies before any send.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real provider-reported usage from an AI API call; mocks can hide tokenizer/usage-accounting mismatches that only appear with the real model endpoint. By rule, execution through real AI API defaults to Agentic unless purely wrapper plumbing; this assertion is about provider model usage behavior itself. Rejected System as lower adjacent level because the critical signal is model/provider usage output, not just generic HTTP mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real calls with representative documents and read provider usage metadata; assert reported total tokens are <= configured budget for each call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry/backoff orchestration between API client wrapper, error classifier, and retry policy. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing only backoff math can miss wiring where retry is not triggered. REAL-INFRA: real network not required; deterministic fake client can emit rate-limit then success. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output content. Rejected System because behavior text is about control flow on a specific error signal, reproducible with in-process stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake provider that returns rate-limit error then success; assert module delays/retries according to policy and returns successful label instead of surfacing the first error.
